analysis.summary: The song is a confident declaration of dominance on the dance floor, urging others to step aside for an undeniable summer heat and smooth bass-driven swagger.

sections.name: Intro
sections.emotion: Mysterious anticipation of a new king taking the throne.

sections.name: Verse 1 / Hook A
sections.emotion: Smooth declaration with a rhythmic push to clear space.

sections.name: Hook B (Repeat)
sections.emotion: Reinforced confidence and catchy minimal groove energy.

sections.name: Post-Hook / Crowd Chant
sections.emotion: Collective hype with deep bass resonance and soft swagger.

sections.name: Groove 2 / Call & Response
sections.emotion: Breathless rhythm focusing on kick/clap/bass interplay.

sections.name: Pre-Hook
sections.emotion: Building tension before the final heat of the room.

sections.name: Hook C (Repeat)
sections.emotion: Peak confidence with a cool summer night finish.

sections.name: Bridge / Breakdown
sections.emotion: Minimalist pause emphasizing the singular presence of 'Move over'.

keywords.imageryPrompt: A glowing warm haze rising from a crowded retro dance floor, illuminating silhouettes with soft orange and pink light against dark walls.
Close up of high-fashion streetwear details like gold chains or designer logos reflecting under neon club lights, exuding effortless coolness.
Low frequency waves visualized as rippling dark purple energy pushing through a minimalist room with concrete floors and sparse furniture.
A slow-motion shot of liquid mercury flowing over a sleek black surface, mirroring the effortless flow of the dance moves.
The confident stride of a dancer in 90s style attire stepping forward with one hand on their hip, casting long shadows under strobe lights.
Motion blur capturing the swift lateral movement of bodies sliding across a dance floor as if clearing space for an arrival.

styleHint: BPM 125, minimal late-80s/early-90s hip-house and Eurodance club groove, stripped-down drum machine, tight four-on-the-floor kick, dry snare/clap, simple funky bass riff, short synth stabs, sparse vocal chops, cool female hype voice, confident male rap, call-and-response hook, dance-floor swagger, summer night energy, stylish not loud, catchy but minimal, retro club mix, clean radio-friendly lyrics, no big EDM drops, no overproduced festival sound, 3:20-3:50.
